privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a synthetically generated orbital mechanics scenario with a parking orbit of 6671.14 km and a target geostationary orbit of 42164.17 km. Theoretical calculations verify that the Hohmann transfer requires ~3.895 km/s of Delta-V and ~5.27 hours, while the Bi-elliptic transfer with an intermediate apogee of 100,000 km requires ~4.258 km/s and ~43.22 hours. Since the orbit ratio is ~6.32, the Hohmann transfer is definitively more fuel-efficient and is correctly identified as the expected recommendation. All metadata bounds for times and Delta-V correctly encapsulate these theoretical values.
